claim: The manuscript was a Cathar liturgical manual for the Endura ritual of assisted suicide, written in a creole of Flemish, Old French, and Old High German
evidence_against: Mixed vocabulary from three languages across centuries. Historical experts found no correspondence with known Cathar practices. The Endura was a fast to death, not the elaborate ritual described. Botanical/astronomical sections make no sense as liturgy. Manuscript post-dates Cathar destruction by over a century.

tests_run.details: Mixed vocabulary from three languages across centuries. Historical experts found no correspondence with known Cathar practices. The Endura was a fast to death, not the elaborate ritual described. Botanical/astronomical sections make no sense as liturgy. Manuscript post-dates Cathar destruction by over a century.

reasoning: Historical attempt by Leo Levitov (1987). Cipher type: polyglot creole (no cipher). Proposed language: Flemish-French-German creole.
source: Levitov, L. 'Solution of the Voynich Manuscript' (1987)
